etymology.description: 寵(寵) (chǒng, "to pamper") + 物(物) (wù, "thing") = an object of pampering -> pet
etymology.interpretation: 寵(寵) (chǒng, "to pamper") suggests an action of showing affection, treating someone or something with great care. 物(物) (wù, "thing") refers to an object or being. Together, 寵物(寵物) creates the idea of an entity that receives affection and pampering, logically leading to the sense of a "pet," which is often cared for and loved as part of the family.

examples.chinese: 我们有一只可爱的宠物狗(我們有一隻可愛的寵物狗)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen yǒu yī zhī kě'ài de chǒngwù gǒu.
examples.english: We have a cute pet dog.

examples.chinese: 小猫是最受欢迎的宠物之一(小貓是最受歡迎的寵物之一)。
examples.pinyin: Xiǎo māo shì zuì shòu huānyíng de chǒngwù zhī yī.
examples.english: Kittens are one of the most popular pets.

examples.chinese: 带宠物去看兽医是很重要的(帶寵物去看獸醫是很重要的)。
examples.pinyin: Dài chǒngwù qù kàn shòuyī shì hěn zhòngyào de.
examples.english: It's important to take pets to the veterinarian.
